jobs.title: Senior Environmental Scientist
jobs.description:
About EHS Support

EHS Support is a specialty environmental consulting firm that provides solutions to complex environmental, health, and safety challenges across a range of market sectors and industries. With experts located throughout the United States, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, the United Kingdom, and South Africa, we form long-lasting partnerships with our clients through a culture of excellence, innovation, work ethic, and integrity. We focus on hiring and retaining talented, motivated people who enjoy collaborating with and delivering value to our clients. Our team does more than identify problems— we provide solutions.

Location:

Brisbane, QLD - flexible home, office and field-based role

About The Role

We are seeking an experienced environmental scientist or engineer to join our Queensland team and play a leading role in the delivery of environmental assessment, compliance, remediation and environmental management projects.

This role is suited to an experienced consultant who enjoys technical problem solving, project leadership and working with clients to develop practical and defensible environmental solutions. The position offers exposure to a broad range of projects and sectors, including energy, resources, infrastructure, manufacturing, chemicals and heavy industry.

While a strong background in contaminated land assessment and management is important, we are particularly interested in adaptable practitioners who can apply their technical skills across a range of environmental disciplines, including compliance, approvals, environmental due diligence, environmental management and remediation.

The preferred location for this role is Brisbane; however, other Queensland locations will be considered for suitably experienced candidates. The role involves a flexible mix of office, home and field-based work, together with periodic travel to project sites throughout Queensland and Australia.

Typical Responsibilities

Lead technical delivery of environmental assessment, compliance, remediation and environmental management projects.
Plan, coordinate and manage environmental investigations, including soil, groundwater, surface water and vapour assessment programs.
Review, interpret and evaluate environmental data and develop technically robust and commercially practical recommendations.
Author and review technical reports and project deliverables, including:
Preliminary and detailed site investigations
Contaminated land assessments
Remediation action plans and validation reports
Environmental management plans
Compliance and monitoring report
Environmental approvals and supporting documentation
Provide technical leadership and quality assurance for work completed by project team members.
Lead the technical delivery and project execution of environmental assessment, compliance, remediation and environmental management projects.
Coordinate and supervise subcontractors and specialist service providers during field investigations and remediation works.
Engage directly with clients, regulators, contractors and other stakeholders to support project delivery and resolve technical issues.
Contribute to proposal preparation, scope development and cost estimation for new opportunities.
Support the development of junior and intermediate staff through technical guidance, coaching and project leadership.
Contribute to technical innovation and continuous improvement initiatives within the business.

As a growing business, this role provides opportunities to expand technical leadership responsibilities, strengthen client relationships, contribute to service development and play a meaningful role in the continued growth of the Queensland practice.

Position Requirements

This opportunity is suited to a motivated and adaptable environmental professional who combines strong technical capability with project leadership and client management skills. The successful candidate will demonstrate:

Strong technical knowledge of contaminated land assessment, remediation and environmental management principles.
Excellent analytical and critical-thinking skills, with the ability to develop practical and defensible solutions to complex environmental challenges.
Exceptional written communication skills and demonstrated experience preparing high-quality technical reports and client deliverables.
Strong verbal communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
Proven ability to manage projects, coordinate multidisciplinary teams and deliver successful outcomes.
Ability to work independently while providing leadership and direction within collaborative project teams.
Sound professional judgement and a practical approach to balancing technical, regulatory and commercial considerations.
A strong commitment to health, safety and environmental performance.

Qualifications For This Role Include

Bachelor's or Master's degree in Soil Science, Geology, Hydrogeology, Environmental Science, Environmental Engineering or a related discipline.
Approximately 7-10 years' experience in environmental consulting or a closely related technical role.
Demonstrated experience undertaking and managing contaminated land investigations, environmental site assessments and remediation projects.
Strong understanding of the ASC NEPM and Queensland contaminated land regulatory framework.
Experience managing environmental field programs, including soil, groundwater and surface water investigations.
Demonstrated experience leading the preparation and review of high-quality technical reports and client deliverables.
Proven project management experience, including management of scope, schedule, budget and project risks.
Experience working across multiple environmental service areas such as compliance, approvals, environmental management, due diligence or remediation would be highly regarded.
Current Australian driver's licence and the ability to work in Australia.

Why Join Us?

Our people are fundamental to our success

At EHS Support, we’re passionate about creating a workplace where our people feel valued, supported, and inspired. We empower our team to deliver innovative, practical solutions for our clients and the environment, while continuously growing their careers in a collaborative and forward‑thinking environment.

As a small, employee‑owned consultancy, you’ll be part of a close‑knit team where your voice matters, your contributions are recognised, and your development is genuinely supported.

What’s in It for You?

Flexible working arrangements to support a healthy work–life balance
A competitive salary package that reflects your skills and expertise
Employee referral rewards
Bonuses to reward you for helping grow our team
An opportunity to participate in revenue sharing
A structured mentoring program designed to support your professional and career development
Paid parental leave to support you and your family
Opportunities to work on diverse and challenging projects that expand your experience and capabilities
A collaborative, employee‑owned business with strong values and accessible leadership
Regular social events and a vibrant team culture that makes coming to work enjoyable
Clear pathways for career progression and growth, tailored to your goals

Join us and build a rewarding career where you can make a real impact—on projects, clients, the environment, and your own professional journey.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Research, Analyst, and Information Technology

Industries

Environmental Services

jobs.title: Senior Air Quality Consultant
jobs.description:
As part of continued growth of our Air Quality team, ERM has a position available for an experienced Senior Consultant to join our Brisbane team.

ERM is a global environmental consultancy with one of the largest group of air quality specialists in Australia. We have a client base comprising major Australian and international companies, as well as federal and state government departments. Our projects (in Australia and overseas) include impact assessment and mitigation, air quality monitoring, air quality assessment, dispersion modelling, odour and dust assessment, emissions inventory development, carbon management, applied meteorology, and regulatory reporting.

You will be part of a growing dynamic international company that clients trust and choose to engage. ERM offers a structured career path with outstanding opportunities. If you are a highly motivated and results-orientated person looking to make your mark in a leading environmental consultancy, then we would like to hear from you!

This is an excellent career opportunity for a senior-level professional looking to advance their career with a global environmental leader, and to make a significant impact in successfully implementing ERM's global strategy.

Role Responsibilities:

Deliver emission estimation inventories, odour and air quality modelling assessments;
Write technical environmental reports, effective proposals, and tenders;
Plan projects with a range of complexity and scale;
Achieve client’s expectations for scope, budget, schedule, and quality;
Lead client-focused air permitting and compliance strategies for industrial facilities, addressing complex regulatory and technical issues while maintaining strong client relationships and supporting operational flexibility;
Develop and expand client relationships that support repeat business, identifying and developing new opportunities;
Capitalize on existing client relationships to expand ERM’s profile and market share in the regional air quality market (through a combination of excellence in technical delivery and business development);
Mentor team members in the proper planning and execution of air quality projects, and conduct independent technical reviews of deliverable work products;
Support the general growth and development of ERM’s global air quality services;
Collaborate with other ERM global practitioners to execute air quality projects.

Requirements:

BS/MS in engineering (chemical, mechanical, environmental), environmental science, or related degree.
8+ years of relevant experience in air quality/compliance consulting.
Well established reputation and recognized technical expertise by the marketplace.
Proven experience completing emission inventories and air quality modelling assessments.
Technical expertise such as WRF modelling, RStudio programming is desirable.
Proven experience in business development for large air quality/regulatory compliance contracts, with the ability to contribute to ERM’s annual sales goals.
Excellent communication (written/verbal), strategic thinking, negotiation, presentation, and “people” skills.
Positive outlook, collaborative, and driven by the success of ERM as a global company.
Authorisation to work in Australia.

Who We Are:

As the world’s leading sustainability consulting firm, ERM is uniquely positioned to contribute to the environment and society through the expertise and energy of our employees worldwide. Sustainability is what we do and is at the heart of both our service offerings and how we operate our business. For our people, our vision means attracting, inspiring, developing and rewarding our people to work with the best clients and on the biggest challenges, thus creating valuable careers. We achieve our vision in a sustainable manner by maintaining and living our ERM beliefs that include Together we Challenge, Expertise is our Superpower, and Impact Ignites us.

ERM does not accept recruiting agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our job’s alias, ERM employees or any other company location. ERM is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Thank you for your interest in ERM.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Quality Assurance

Industries

Environmental Services, Civil Engineering, and Oil and Gas
